meta.title: Federal SAVE Act — Computational Amicus Explorer

federal_save_act_challenger_model.name: challenger-bridge-right-to-vote
federal_save_act_challenger_model.label: BRIDGE_RULE
federal_save_act_challenger_model.clause_text: nor shall any person be... deprived of life, liberty, or property, without due process of law

federal_save_act_challenger_model.name: challenger-bridge-regulation-invalid
federal_save_act_challenger_model.label: BRIDGE_RULE
federal_save_act_challenger_model.clause_text: Anderson-Burdick balancing: severe burden + no sufficient justification = invalid regulation

federal_save_act_challenger_model.clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a is a person

federal_save_act_challenger_model.label: SCENARIO_FACT
federal_save_act_challenger_model.clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a is a U.S. citizen

federal_save_act_challenger_model.name: challenger-scenario-eligible
federal_save_act_challenger_model.label: SCENARIO_FACT
federal_save_act_challenger_model.clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a is eligible to vote

federal_save_act_challenger_model.name: challenger-scenario-application
federal_save_act_challenger_model.label: SCENARIO_FACT
federal_save_act_challenger_model.clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: registration-attempt-a is a voter registration application

federal_save_act_challenger_model.name: challenger-scenario-attempts-to-register
federal_save_act_challenger_model.label: SCENARIO_FACT
federal_save_act_challenger_model.clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a attempts to register via registration-attempt-a

federal_save_act_challenger_model.name: challenger-scenario-no-documentary-proof
federal_save_act_challenger_model.label: SCENARIO_FACT
federal_save_act_challenger_model.clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a does not possess documentary proof

federal_save_act_challenger_model.name: challenger-scenario-no-presentation
federal_save_act_challenger_model.label: SCENARIO_FACT
federal_save_act_challenger_model.clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a does not present documentary proof with application

federal_save_act_challenger_model.name: challenger-scenario-no-fault
federal_save_act_challenger_model.label: EMPIRICAL_ASSUMPTION
federal_save_act_challenger_model.clause_text: approximately 18,000 applicants were unable to complete registration due to the documentary proof requirement

federal_save_act_challenger_model.name: challenger-scenario-material-burden
federal_save_act_challenger_model.label: EMPIRICAL_ASSUMPTION
federal_save_act_challenger_model.clause_text: the burden of obtaining a birth certificate... will be nontrivial for some voters

federal_save_act_challenger_model.name: challenger-scenario-alternative-process-denied
federal_save_act_challenger_model.label: INTERPRETATION_CHALLENGER
federal_save_act_challenger_model.clause_text: such official shall make a determination — interpretation: the determination is discretionary and may result in denial

federal_save_act_facts.name: text-save-act-is-law
federal_save_act_facts.label: TEXT_FACT
federal_save_act_facts.clause_text: This Act may be cited as the "Safeguard American Voter Eligibility Act" or the "SAVE Act".

federal_save_act_facts.name: text-save-act-documentary-proof-requirement
federal_save_act_facts.label: PROHIBITION
federal_save_act_facts.clause_text: the State shall not accept and process an application to register to vote in an election for Federal office unless the applicant presents documentary proof of United States citizenship with the application

federal_save_act_facts.name: text-documentary-proof-from-qualifying-documents
federal_save_act_facts.label: BRIDGE_RULE
federal_save_act_facts.clause_text: the term 'documentary proof of United States citizenship' means any of the following: (1) REAL ID... (2) valid United States passport... (3) military ID... (4) govt photo ID showing US birth... (5) govt photo ID with supporting doc... (6) certified birth certificate... (7) naturalization certificate

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-bridge-defense-validates
federal_save_act_government_model.label: BRIDGE_RULE
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: Crawford: regulation satisfying Anderson-Burdick is constitutionally valid

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-election-integrity-interest
federal_save_act_government_model.label: DOCTRINAL_RULE
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: there is no question about the legitimacy or importance of the State's interest in counting only the votes of eligible voters

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-important-interest
federal_save_act_government_model.label: DOCTRINAL_RULE
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: Crawford plurality: election integrity is an important governmental interest

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-reasonable-requirement
federal_save_act_government_model.label: INTERPRETATION_GOVERNMENT
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: for most voters who need them, the inconvenience of making a trip to the BMV... does not qualify as a substantial burden

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-procedure-evenhanded
federal_save_act_government_model.label: INTERPRETATION_GOVERNMENT
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: when an election regulation imposes only reasonable, nondiscriminatory restrictions... the State's important regulatory interests are generally sufficient

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-rationally-connected
federal_save_act_government_model.label: INTERPRETATION_GOVERNMENT
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: the ID requirement is amply justified by the valid interest in protecting the integrity and reliability of the electoral process

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-adequate-alternative
federal_save_act_government_model.label: INTERPRETATION_GOVERNMENT
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: each State shall establish a process under which an applicant who cannot provide documentary proof... may submit such other evidence — interpretation: the process provides an adequate safety valve

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-burden-not-severe
federal_save_act_government_model.label: EMPIRICAL_ASSUMPTION
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: the inconvenience... does not qualify as a substantial burden on the right to vote

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-scenario-person
federal_save_act_government_model.label: SCENARIO_FACT
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a is a person

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-scenario-citizen
federal_save_act_government_model.label: SCENARIO_FACT
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a is a U.S. citizen

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-scenario-eligible
federal_save_act_government_model.label: SCENARIO_FACT
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a is eligible to vote

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-scenario-application
federal_save_act_government_model.label: SCENARIO_FACT
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: registration-attempt-a is a voter registration application

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-scenario-attempts-to-register
federal_save_act_government_model.label: SCENARIO_FACT
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a attempts to register via registration-attempt-a

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-assume-right-to-vote-arguendo
federal_save_act_government_model.label: INTERPRETATION_GOVERNMENT
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: Government concedes arguendo that citizen-a has a protected right to vote

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-scenario-no-documentary-proof
federal_save_act_government_model.label: SCENARIO_FACT
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a lacks documentary proof (conceded)

federal_save_act_government_model.name: government-scenario-alternative-process-approved
federal_save_act_government_model.label: INTERPRETATION_GOVERNMENT
federal_save_act_government_model.clause_text: the process provides a guaranteed path to registration — interpretation: mandatory approval when evidence satisfies standards

federal_save_act_hinge_discretionary.name: semantic-b-discretionary-denial
federal_save_act_hinge_discretionary.label: INTERPRETIVE_ASSUMPTION
federal_save_act_hinge_discretionary.clause_text: "shall make a determination as to whether the applicant has sufficiently established" — interpretation: duty to decide, but determination may be denial

federal_save_act_hinge_mandatory.name: semantic-a-mandatory-approval
federal_save_act_hinge_mandatory.label: INTERPRETIVE_ASSUMPTION
federal_save_act_hinge_mandatory.clause_text: "shall make a determination" — interpretation: mandatory duty to approve when attestation + evidence satisfies EAC standards

nodes.label: H.R. 22 — SAVE Act
nodes.description: Safeguard American Voter Eligibility Act, 119th Congress. Passed House 220-208, April 10, 2025.

nodes.label: NVRA Baseline (52 U.S.C. §§ 20501–20511)
nodes.description: National Voter Registration Act — the statutory baseline that the SAVE Act amends.

nodes.label: 5th Amendment (Due Process / Equal Protection)
nodes.description: Equal protection component applied to federal action via Bolling v. Sharpe, 347 U.S. 497 (1954). Operative constitutional hook for the model.

nodes.label: 14th Amendment § 1 (Citizenship / Equal Protection)
nodes.description: Defines citizenship and equal protection for state action. Provides the citizenship definition used in voter qualification analysis.

nodes.label: 17th Amendment (Senate Voter Qualifications)
nodes.description: Extends voter qualification framework to Senate elections.

nodes.label: 24th Amendment (Poll Tax Prohibition)
nodes.description: Prohibits poll taxes in federal elections. Relevant to whether document-acquisition costs constitute an impermissible tax.

nodes.label: Crawford v. Marion County (2008)
nodes.description: Supreme Court plurality upholding Indiana voter ID law under Anderson-Burdick framework. Key precedent for burden analysis.

nodes.label: Anderson-Burdick Framework
nodes.description: Sliding-scale test: severe burdens trigger strict scrutiny; lesser burdens require only rational basis.
nodes.source_ref: Anderson v. Celebrezze, 460 U.S. 780 (1983); Burdick v. Takushi, 504 U.S. 428 (1992)

nodes.label: Fish v. Kobach (10th Cir. 2016)
nodes.description: Kansas documentary proof-of-citizenship requirement. Found ~18,000 applicants unable to complete registration. Key empirical record.

nodes.label: Bolling v. Sharpe (1954)
nodes.description: Reverse incorporation: Fifth Amendment due process includes equal protection component binding the federal government.

nodes.label: source_manifest.json
nodes.description: Registry of 21 authoritative sources with URLs, citations, and local file references.

nodes.label: clause_trace.csv
nodes.description: 38-row mapping from every defaxiom to its authoritative source clause.

nodes.label: ACE Controlled-English Statements
nodes.description: 13 source provisions translated to Attempto Controlled English with predicate targets.

nodes.label: citizen-a is a qualified voter
nodes.description: Scenario ground truth: citizen-a is a U.S. citizen, eligible to vote, of legal age.

nodes.label: citizen-a lacks qualifying documents
nodes.description: citizen-a does not possess any of the qualifying documents listed in the SAVE Act (no REAL ID, passport, birth certificate, etc.).

nodes.label: Voting is a constitutionally protected right
nodes.description: Source-traced from constitutional provisions.

nodes.label: SAVE Act is a federal law
nodes.description: Source-traced: H.R. 22 is a law regulating voter registration.

nodes.label: Registration attempt is a registration transaction
nodes.description: The act of applying to register constitutes a registration transaction subject to SAVE Act requirements.

nodes.label: Statute triggers denial for citizen-a
nodes.description: Under the SAVE Act, citizen-a's registration is denied because they lack documentary proof.

nodes.label: Citizen lacks documents through no fault
nodes.description: Challenger-side empirical premise: citizen-a lacks qualifying documents through no personal fault (e.g., born at home in rural area, elderly, records destroyed).

nodes.label: Obtaining documents is materially burdensome
nodes.description: Challenger-side empirical premise: obtaining qualifying documents imposes a material burden (cost, travel, time, bureaucratic barriers).

nodes.label: Government: burden is not severe
nodes.description: Government-side empirical premise: the burden of obtaining documents is not severe, and an adequate alternative process exists.

nodes.label: Adequate alternative process exists
nodes.description: Government premise: the § 8(j)(2)(A) attestation process provides an adequate alternative path to registration.

nodes.label: Mandatory alternative-process reading
nodes.description: § 8(j)(2)(A): 'sufficiently established' means the official must approve if evidence is adequate → guaranteed path to registration.

nodes.label: Discretionary alternative-process reading
nodes.description: § 8(j)(2)(A): 'sufficiently established' gives the official discretion → possible denial even with evidence → no guaranteed registration path.

nodes.label: Election integrity is an important interest
nodes.description: Government doctrinal premise: preventing noncitizen voting is an important or compelling government interest.

nodes.label: Requirement is reasonable and evenhanded
nodes.description: Government doctrinal premise: the documentary proof requirement is reasonable, rationally connected to the interest, and evenhanded.

nodes.label: Severe burden defeats regulation
nodes.description: Challenger doctrinal premise: under Anderson-Burdick, a severe burden on the right to vote makes the regulation constitutionally invalid.

nodes.label: Registration State Machine
nodes.description: 10-state, 9-event executable model: :submitted → :pending → :approved → :registered or :denied, with :alt-pending → :alt-approved/:alt-denied alternative path.

nodes.label: Document Recognizer Model
nodes.description: Executable recognizers for REAL ID, passport, military ID, government photo ID, and supporting documents.

nodes.label: Burden Derivation Chain (defun)
nodes.description: 5-step executable burden chain: lacks-docs → cannot-obtain → material-burden → severe-burden → invalid-regulation.

nodes.label: Interpretive Hinge Fork
nodes.description: Encapsulate-based model: § 8(j)(2)(A) 'sufficiently established' splits into mandatory (government) vs. discretionary (challenger) readings.

nodes.label: Existential Propositions (defun-sk)
nodes.description: 4 Skolemized existential claims: exists-burdened-citizen, exists-no-fault-citizen, exists-documentless-eligible-voter, exists-citizen-facing-discretionary-denial.

nodes.label: Anderson-Burdick Doctrinal Standard
nodes.description: Encapsulate-based doctrinal standard: severe burden → strict scrutiny; not-severe → rational basis.

nodes.label: Denial derivation
nodes.description: citizen-a's registration is denied because they lack documentary proof and no alternative process is approved.

nodes.label: Lacks-documents derivation
nodes.description: citizen-a possesses no qualifying document from the SAVE Act list.

nodes.label: Full burden derivation chain
nodes.description: Executable defun chain: citizen-a-has-material-burden → citizen-a-burden-is-severe → regulation-is-invalid.

nodes.label: Alternative-process derivation
nodes.description: Under mandatory reading: alternative process provides guaranteed registration path. Under discretionary reading: official may deny.

nodes.label: Interpretive hinge fork split
nodes.description: The outcome pivots on whether § 8(j)(2)(A) is read as mandatory approval or discretionary determination.

nodes.label: registered-implies-prior-acceptance-path
nodes.description: Registration requires a prior acceptance path through the state machine. 0 axioms.

nodes.label: denied-implies-prior-denial-path
nodes.description: Denial requires a prior denial path through the state machine. 0 axioms.

nodes.label: terminal-state-remains-terminal
nodes.description: Once registered or denied, later events do not change the terminal outcome. 0 axioms.

nodes.label: all-nonqualifying-implies-no-documentary-proof
nodes.description: A bundle of only nonqualifying documents cannot satisfy documentary proof. 0 axioms.

nodes.label: conflict-condition-pivots-on-valid-regulation
nodes.description: The constitutional conflict condition is logically equivalent to (not (valid-regulationp law x)). The entire dispute reduces to this single predicate.

nodes.label: full-burden-chain
nodes.description: Derives: citizen-a faces severe burden → regulation is invalid, using the executable defun chain.

nodes.label: invalid-regulation-enables-conflict-condition
nodes.description: If the regulation is not valid, the formal conflict condition is established.

nodes.label: valid-regulation-negates-conflict-condition
nodes.description: If the regulation is valid, no formal conflict condition exists.

nodes.label: valid-regulationp
nodes.description: The single interpretive/doctrinal predicate that determines the constitutional outcome. Not proved by ACL2 — determined by the assumption set.

nodes.label: Challenger: CONSTITUTIONAL CONFLICT
nodes.description: Under challenger assumptions (no-fault, material burden, severe burden, discretionary reading, invalid regulation), ACL2 derives that a formal constitutional conflict exists.

nodes.label: Government: NO CONFLICT
nodes.description: Under government assumptions (burden not severe, adequate alternative, mandatory reading, election integrity, valid regulation), ACL2 derives that no formal constitutional conflict exists.

hypotheticals.label: Citizen lacks documents through no fault
Obtaining qualifying documents is materially burdensome
Election integrity is an important government interest
